channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Allianz Unit to Cut as Many as 1,800 Jobs in Push to Adopt AI
item.description: Related: AI’s Impact: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Allianz Partners, which employs more than 22,000 people, has previously warned that staffing levels could be impacted by AI-driven transformations. Related: OpenAI’s Altman Says AI Unlikely to Lead to ‘Jobs Apocalypse’ Munich Re’s primary insurance unit Ergo aims to cut about 1,000 positions in Germany partly as a result of its increased use of artificial intelligence, Bloomberg has reported. Allianz SE plans to eliminate hundreds of positions at its assistance and travel insurance subsidiary Allianz Partners as the company pushes forward with implementing artificial intelligence solutions.

item.title: RGA inks block life deals with Sumitomo Life, Anshin Life
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Supply shocks to slow global premium growth as fragmentation reshapes risk landscape: Swiss Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA appoints new members to the Secondary Markets Advisory Committee
item.description: The FCA has appointed members to its advisory committee on secondary markets for the period July 2026 to July 2028. It supports the work of the FCA by: The Secondary Markets Advisory Committee members are: The committee will be chaired by Jon Relleen (Director of Infrastructure & Exchanges) and the Trading Policy team provides secretariat support. The Secondary Markets Advisory Committee supports the FCA’s work in wholesale secondary markets in equities, fixed income, foreign exchange, and commodities markets across securities, futures, swaps, and options markets.

item.title: Showing our workings: solving for growth, risk and trust
item.description: I was struck by how differently teachers approach problem-solving now: with creativity. Because the problems in front of us are big, including the UK’s productivity and growth challenge. Speech by Sarah Pritchard, deputy chief executive, at a Breakfast Briefing at The Whitehall Industry Group.

item.title: Your SaaS Metrics Are A Result, Not A Strategy
item.description: They are the result of strategy — or sometimes the result of a lack of it. LTV/CAC is one of the most important SaaS metrics. But they do not shine a light on strategy.

item.title: Familiar Names Top Active US Investor Ranks In Q2
item.description: All are names that repeatedly show up high in active investor rankings. Below, we ranked the 19 most active investors for Q2: When we narrow the ranks to only lead investors in venture rounds, the lineup changes some, but not dramatically. Per Crunchbase data, General Catalyst and Andreessen Horowitz stood out among the most active U.S.

item.title: Depth of non-life soft market downturn to be more limited this time: Swiss Re
item.description: Swiss Re has said that while the non-life insurance and reinsurance market has entered a softer phase of the underwriting cycle, it does not anticipate the depth of the downturn being as significant as we’ve seen in the past. On prospects for the global re/insurance market Swiss Re explains that, “Global non-life real premium growth is expected to soften to 0.6% in 2026, reflecting competitive pricing conditions and moderating economic momentum, while life insurance growth will stay robust at 2.3%, supported by higher yields.” At the same time, development of artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure and the afore-mentioned geopolitical fragmentation, are driving new demand for resilience and risk transfer solutions, Swiss Re said. These interconnected exposures call for solutions that go beyond traditional insurance, combining risk engineering, alternative risk transfer and financing to help businesses invest with greater resilience.” On non-life insurance premium, Swiss Re notes this sector is “entering a softer phase of the underwriting cycle.” But the reinsurance company noted that, “this cycle is unfolding in a different environment from previous ones.” Leading Swiss Re to suggest that, “Rising claims inflation, geopolitical uncertainty and growing catastrophe exposures are likely to limit the depth of the downturn.”

item.title: Oxbridge Re closes five tokenized reinsurance security offerings, raising $7.1m on Solana
item.description: Oxbridge Re Holdings Ltd., the Cayman Islands-based reinsurance company, along with its subsidiary SurancePlus, has successfully closed five private placements of tokenized reinsurance securities on Solana blockchain, with total gross proceeds of combined offerings amounting to approximately $7.1 million. The tokenized offerings include three tokenized reinsurance securities providing qualified investors with synthetic contractual exposure to designated reinsurance risk associated with HCI Group, Inc.’s Fortex Reinsurance SPC, Ltd., together with Oxbridge Re’s traditional tokenized reinsurance offerings. We reported last month that HCI Group was tapping into the wave of interest in taking risk on-chain and issuing tokenized reinsurance securities as a way to access risk capital through a partnership with Oxbridge Re.

item.title: Ambassador mutual cat bond and ILW fund nears $900m in assets
item.description: When we last reported on Embassy’s Ambassador mutual cat bond and ILS fund strategy back in January , it had grown its total net assets under management to a new high of just over US $700 million, while the strategy delivered an 11.61% return for the last year of record. The Ambassador US mutual fund strategy operated by Embassy Asset Management that allocates to catastrophe bonds and private insurance-linked securities (ILS) that we believe to be largely ILW’s, has grown its assets under management further to reach almost $900 million as of this week. The Ambassador Fund has been growing steadily for Embassy Asset Management, further demonstrating the attraction mutual fund investors have to the catastrophe bond and related ILS investments.

item.title: RGA expands Japan reinsurance activity through two coinsurance agreements
item.description: RGA, a global life and health reinsurance company supporting insurers with risk management and financial solutions, has completed two block coinsurance agreements in Japan with Sumitomo Life Insurance Company and Tokio Marine & Nichido Life Insurance Co., Ltd. RGA said the agreements demonstrate the increasing role of coinsurance arrangements in Japan, as insurers explore additional options for managing in-force portfolios, improving capital efficiency and responding to evolving business requirements. (Anshin Life) involving the reinsurance of a block of whole life policies through a coinsurance structure.

item.title: Aon reveals new leadership structure for Reinsurance in UK and Ireland
item.description: This new leadership structure strengthens our ability to bring the best of Aon to clients across the UK and Ireland, enhancing our focus on growth, innovation and operational excellence.” Large, global insurance and reinsurance broking group Aon has expanded Tom Murray’s role to Head of Reinsurance for the UK and Ireland, and appointed new leaders within the team to support the efficient delivery of solutions and services to clients. Effective immediately, Aon has appointed Andrew Buckland as Chief Operating Officer of the Reinsurance UK and Ireland team.

item.title: Acrisure launches new service for UK high-net-worth individuals and families
item.description: Acrisure, a global fintech company offering insurance, reinsurance, real estate and financial services, has launched Acrisure Private Office, designed to support high-net-worth individuals and families in the UK dealing with complex personal insurance needs. This initiative follows Acrisure’s acquisition of Smith Greenfield earlier this year, integrating specialised private client expertise into the company. By offering a single, trusted relationship to manage and coordinate all insurance requirements, Acrisure Private Office provides clients with the tailored care of a boutique adviser alongside the extensive backing of a global enterprise.

item.title: Connecticut Seeks Consultant to Explore Nonprofit Liability Insurance Options
item.description: The Connecticut Insurance Department (CID) is seeking a consultant to conduct a study and present options to improve the liability insurance market for nonprofit organizations operating in the state. A report issued in January by a working group of legislators, brokers, insurers, regulators, and nonprofit leaders found that the nonprofit human services sector is experiencing rising costs and reduced availability of liability and other property/casualty insurance coverage. Key findings of the Nonprofit Liability, Property & Casualty Insurance Working Group were: According to the working group report , organizations serving higher‑risk populations—particularly social services, behavioral health, foster care, residential programs, and justice‑involved populations—face increasing difficulty obtaining affordable and adequate coverage.

item.title: Firefighters Killed in Colorado Remembered for Bravery as Wildfires Burn in West
item.description: The three firefighters killed on June 27 in western Colorado were members of a Helitack crew that sometimes drops into remote areas by helicopters. With wildfires burning across many Western states, wildland firefighters gathered to pay tribute to three of their own who died after they were trapped by flames a week ago. Wildland Fire Service Chief Brian Fennessy said during a memorial service in Grand Junction, Colorado, near where the firefighters died while battling flames on the Colorado-Utah border.

item.title: Labor Crunch Tests Growth Limits for US Data Center Builders
item.description: Following several quarters of record order books, a skilled-labor shortage is testing the limits of growth for the craft-labor providers who build data centers across the US. Revenue growth for major craft‑labor suppliers including Sterling Infrastructure Inc., MasTec Inc., Quanta Services Inc., Emcor Group Inc. That reflects how a tight labor market, rather than any cooling in demand, is constraining expansion, Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Scott Levine said.

item.title: Norm Ai Raises $120 Million Series C at $1.2 Billion Valuation to Expand Legal and Regulatory AI
item.description: The post Norm Ai Raises $120 Million Series C at $1.2 Billion Valuation to Expand Legal and Regulatory AI appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Norm Ai, an AI company focused on legal and regulatory automation, has raised a $120 million Series C round at a $1.2 billion valuation, bringing its total funding to $256 million. Norm Ai, an AI company focused on legal and regulatory automation, has raised a $120 million Series C round at a $1.2 billion valuation, bringing its total funding to $256 million.

item.title: Insurity Expands Strategic Partnership with OIP Insurtech to Strengthen Technology Delivery and Implementation Support
item.description: The post Insurity Expands Strategic Partnership with OIP Insurtech to Strengthen Technology Delivery and Implementation Support appeared first on Insurtech Insights . The Partnership Insurity, a provider of cloud-based software for insurance carriers, MGAs, and brokers, announced the expansion of its strategic partnership with OIP Insurtech, a technology company serving the insurtech and insurance market through software development, automation, staff augmentation, and IT support services. Since forming their partnership in 2023, Insurity and OIP Insurtech have worked together to enhance product delivery, expand implementation and support capabilities, and deliver AI-enabled document intelligence and system integration services across the insurance ecosystem.

item.title: HUB names Samuel Garvey Commercial Lines Analytics Practice Leader
item.description: HUB International has appointed Samuel Garvey as Commercial Lines Analytics Practice Leader, expanding the firm’s data and analytics capabilities across the U.S. He will also work with commercial lines teams to embed analytics across new business and client retention efforts. Garvey joins HUB from an independent insurance brokerage, where he served as Vice President of Analytics and Team Lead, overseeing actuarial, captive, and catastrophe modeling functions while helping develop a commercial insurtech platform.

item.title: MarineMax expands distribution opportunities for financing & insurance offerings
item.description: The post MarineMax expands distribution opportunities for financing & insurance offerings appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . “This partnership supports an important strategic objective for MarineMax to expand the reach of our higher-margin financing and insurance offerings. Through the partnership, MarineMax expects to broaden the distribution opportunities for its Newcoast Financial Services subsidiary by engaging with a larger network of boat buyers and transactions across the pre-owned marine marketplace.

item.title: Mumbai Struggles to Cope as Record Rains Lash Financial Hub
item.description: Mumbai’s wettest start to July in decades killed at least 13 people in the city and its adjoining areas and caused widespread disruption for millions of residents, raising questions about the quality of infrastructure in India’s financial hub. The country’s weather office has so far issued an unprecedented four consecutive ‘red alert’ notifications — the highest weather severity warning — for Mumbai and its adjoining areas over the past seven days. Mumbai and its surroundings recorded heaviest early-monsoon rainfall since at least 2000 despite a delayed onset linked to El Niño conditions.

item.title: Los Angeles Heat Builds Ahead of Hot Spell Across US Southwest
item.description: A heat advisory will also take effect Thursday across parts of Southern California, including Los Angeles and Burbank, the agency said. Temperatures are set to rise across Los Angeles and parts of the southwestern U.S. Extreme heat is already settling across Southern California and Arizona as readings threaten to reach 116F (47C) across the region, including in Palm Springs and Phoenix through Thursday, according to the National Weather Service.

item.title: Florida Supreme Court Ruling Could Mean New Pressure to Settle High-Dollar Lawsuits
item.description: He added that defense lawyers must now “be more vigilant in identifying cases with punitive damages potential at the outset and in developing strategies later in litigation, such as aggressive dispositive motions, to address that exposure before trial.” The state Supreme Court ruling also means that defendants in lawsuits will face early exposure to discovery of their financial worth, “which is a powerful litigation tool that can create significant settlement pressure,” Carey noted. The Perlmutter opinion may now mean more pressure from insurance carriers on their clients to settle some lawsuits earlier, in order to avoid protracted litigation, hefty defense costs, and big damage awards. The Florida Supreme Court in June answered a certified question from the lower court, settling conflicting rulings from state appellate courts about how much evidence evidence for punitive damages should be presented early in litigation proceedings.

item.title: Hong Kong captive tally climbs to 9 with pair of new approvals
item.description: Hong Kong's Insurance Authority (HKIA) has authorised two new captive insurers — HSH Captive, established by The Hongkong and Shanghai Hotels, and SF Captive, set up by SF Holding. Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: QBE Australia Pacific chief executive Sue Houghton to retire
item.description: QBE Insurance Group has announced that Australia Pacific chief executive Sue Houghton intends to retire from QBE at the end of 2026, the company said in a stock exchange announcement. Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: Natasha Golden to lead WTW-backed Zest Insurance in Australia
item.description: Zest Insurance, the digital insurance platform launched by WTW to target small and medium enterprises in Australia, has appointed Natasha Golden as its new head, according to a LinkedIn update. Your company may already have a corporate license in place.

item.title: Opening remarks for the FSB 2026 ReSolve Event: Exploring cross-sectoral interconnections and financial stability implications
item.description: It is a real pleasure to welcome you all today at this Financial Stability Board (FSB) ReSolve event. For the first time, resolution authorities and members of the different CBCMs are meeting in a truly cross-sectoral setting. That matters because financial crises do not respect sectoral boundaries, and neither should our preparedness.

item.title: Hannover Re structure Kaith Re issues $7.47m LI Re 2026-3 private cat bond
item.description: Hannover Re, the global reinsurance company, has facilitated issuance of a new $7.47 million private LI Re 2026-3 catastrophe bond using its Bermuda-domiciled transformer vehicle Kaith Re Ltd., with this new deal expected to support a collateralized, transformed and securitized reinsurance or retrocession arrangement. In 2026, we’ve now reported on $95 million of issuance under the Seaside Re program from Kaith Re, while the structure also issued Hannover Re’s most recent $35 million Cumulus Re cloud outage cyber cat bond deal earlier this year and now issuance under the LI Re program has risen to $11.47 million of notes. This Series 2026-3 issuance is the third LI Re private catastrophe bond to be issued by Kaith Re Ltd.

item.title: Porch targets lower pricing for debut $100m Harbor Crest Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: Porch Group, the US homeowners specialist insurer, is now targeting lower pricing for its debut catastrophe bond sponsorship, still aiming to secure $100 million in multi-peril collateralized catastrophe reinsurance through the Harbor Crest Re Ltd. The notes will cover Porch against certain losses from named storms, winter storms, severe weather events, wildfire and fire-following earthquake events across the 50 states of the US and D.C., and have been structured on a per-occurrence and indemnity trigger basis The $100 million of Harbor Crest Re Series 2026-1 Class A notes come with an initial base expected loss of 1.97%. So far, 2026 has seen 12 first time cat bond sponsors enter the market in H1 , with Porch set to make that 13 once its debut cat bond settles.

item.title: HDI Global strengthens US Credit and Political Risk operations with Amy Shinkman appointment
item.description: Corporate and specialty insurer HDI Global (HDI), a provider of commercial insurance and risk management solutions for businesses worldwide, has appointed Amy Shinkman as Credit and Political Risk Lead for its US operations. In the newly created role, Shinkman will be based in Washington, DC, where she will oversee the continued expansion of HDI Global’s credit and political risk business across the US market. Shinkman joins the business with 25 years of experience in global credit and political risk underwriting.

item.title: Quotech and Provectus deliver AI-powered underwriting tech to Convex’s new MGA platform
item.description: Quotech, a developer of integrated technology solutions for underwriting and broking teams, partners with Provectus, an AI systems integrator, to develop new AI-powered underwriting tools for MGA Kinetic Insurance Services. Theo Butt, Chief Executive Officer at Kinetic, said: “Convex was originally attracted to Quotech by the team’s ability to incorporate real-world underwriting experience into technology for insurance underwriting. “Both have delivered outstanding results for Convex on previous projects, so bringing them together to build the underwriting platform for Kinetic, our new MGA platform focused on large, complex, and middle-market specialty risks, was an obvious choice.

item.title: Gallagher strengthens Switzerland team with two new leadership appointments
item.description: In her role at Gallagher, the executive will lead the team responsible for developing distribution and client engagement across Switzerland with a focus on new business development. International insurance broker Gallagher has announced the appointment of two new executives to be based in Switzerland: Gosia Kuczewska and Samuel Trost. Both Kuczewska and Trost will report to CEO of Gallagher in Switzerland, Christian Peters, who commented: “Gosia and Samuel will both play a pivotal role in growing our Swiss client base and by creating these distinct roles we will be well placed to deliver on our growth aspirations.

item.title: Sequel Insurance Agencies Acquires Texas’ Watkins
item.description: Sequel Insurance Agencies, powered by SIAA, a leading independent insurance agency platform and perpetuation partner for entrepreneurial agencies, announced its acquisition of Watkins Insurance Group including five office locations across Austin and Central Texas. This transaction marks another strategic milestone in Sequel’s continued growth and strengthens its presence in Austin and the Texas market, incorporating Watkins Insurance Group’s exceptional reputation for delivering personalized insurance and risk management solutions. Topics Mergers & Acquisitions Texas Agencies

item.title: Supreme Court Lets Texas’ Age App Verification Law Stand
item.description: A trade association asked the Supreme Court to block the Texas law while the 5th Circuit weighed the state’s appeal. Supreme Court declined Monday to intervene. Circuit Court of Appeals in late May allowed Texas to enforce the law until its judges issue a ruling on the law’s constitutionality.

item.title: Allstate Sued by Oklahoma for Alleged Scheme to Underpay Claims
item.description: In Oklahoma, Drummond claims that third-party engineering firms and adjusting companies participated in the alleged scheme with Allstate, providing reports that supported predetermined objectives to deny or reduce claims. Oklahoma’s attorney general sued Allstate Corp., alleging the insurer schemed to minimize payouts to homeowners for wind and hailstorm damage in order to boost its profits. The state’s top lawyer, Gentner Drummond, alleges that Allstate implemented an internal program to drastically deny or minimize payments of legitimate covered losses, according to the lawsuit filed Tuesday in state court in Cleveland.

item.title: Gift City’s new MGA regime a ‘build-or-broker’ moment for Indian insurance
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: SoftBank forms SB Neo to launch AI cloud business in the US
item.description: The announcement follows SoftBank’s broader push into AI infrastructure, including plans to launch its AI Data Center GPU Cloud service in October 2026 and develop gigawatt-scale AI data centers in Japan. and SoftBank Group have established SB Neo, a new US-based company that will provide AI cloud infrastructure to enterprise customers. SoftBank said the initiative builds on its AI cloud efforts in Japan, where it began offering a beta version of its GPU cloud service powered by its Infrinia AI Cloud OS software stack in May 2026.

item.title: Principal to acquire Beam Benefits
item.description: Principal Financial Group announced an agreement to acquire Beam Benefits, an employee benefits company serving over 25,000 small businesses. Beam’s talent and deep expertise in the small business marketplace will be additive to our SMB strategy.” – Amy Friedrich, president of Benefits and Protection at Principal. Joining forces with Principal is the natural next step in our journey.” – Tolithia Kornweibel, CEO of Beam Benefits.

item.title: APCO Holdings acquires Georgia Wheelz
item.description: APCO Holdings has acquired the assets of Georgia Wheelz, an F&I agency serving independent automotive dealers across the Southeast. Following the acquisition, the Georgia Wheelz team will join APCO’s GWC Warranty sales organization, expanding GWC’s presence in the independent dealer market. Founded in 2017 and based in Eatonton, Georgia, Georgia Wheelz provides F&I products, dealer training, and performance support to independent dealerships.

item.title: Duck Creek Acquires Send
item.description: (Image source: Send homepage.) Duck Creek Technologies (Boston) has acquired Send Technology Solutions Ltd. Duck Creek says the acquisition brings together its core insurance platform and Agentic AI Platform with Send’s AI-native underwriting orchestration engine, creating a more connected underwriting-to-core operating model for commercial, specialty and London Market insurers. Supporting Complex Commercial and Specialty Underwriting Duck Creek says Send supports more than $26 billion in gross written premium through its underwriting orchestration engine.

item.title: Meta Says States Seeking $1.4 Trillion in Penalties in August Youth Safety Trial
item.description: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on Monday that four states were seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled the public about their safety. Meta put forward the figure in its response to the attorneys general’s filings on how penalties should be calculated if the states prevailed at trial. The number, which has not previously been disclosed and is close to Meta’s market capitalization of around $1.5 trillion, comes ahead of an August trial in Oakland, California, over the claims brought by California, Colorado, Kentucky and New Jersey against the company.

item.title: Global Cargo Theft Ring Charged in $10M Scheme
item.description: Eight individuals were charged with conspiracy to transport and possess stolen property in connection with an international, organized scheme to steal cargo from commercial shippers, according to a joint announcement last week by the United States Attorney for the Southern District of New York and the New York Field Office of the Federal Bureau of Investigation. From March 2023 to the present, the defendants carried out a large-scale, organized scheme to steal cargo from commercial shippers. “Organized cargo theft is an attack on the integrity of our nation’s commercial supply chain and our markets more generally.

item.title: U.S. Proposes Dropping Brake Pedal Requirements for Self-Driving Vehicles
item.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration proposed on Thursday to end a government requirement for manual brake pedals in self-driving vehicles, a move that would make it easier to deploy such vehicles on U.S. The proposal would not apply to vehicles with human driver controls and NHTSA said it would not drop braking performance requirements, including strict stopping distance standards for self-driving vehicles. Under the law, ⁠fully self-driving vehicles do not need NHTSA approval if they have required human controls, like steering wheels, brake pedals or mirrors.

item.title: Sompo Acquiring Workers Comp Writer Service Insurance Companies
item.description: According to the announcement, the acquisition of Service Insurance Companies will broaden Sompo’s underwriting capabilities and presence across products, customer segments and distribution channels within the small and medium-sized enterprise segment, which is a focus for workers comp specialist being acquired. “Together with Service Insurance Companies’ seasoned leadership team and proven workers compensation platform, Sompo is establishing a top-tier flagship for our workers compensation business,” said Chris Sparro, CEO, North America, Sompo Commercial P&C Insurance, in a media statement. “Together with Sompo, Service Insurance Companies is better positioned to extend the reach from a monoline workers compensation provider into a diversified commercial insurance organization with enhanced scale, capabilities and amplified market relevance,” Davis said.

item.title: Sompo swoops for US workers’ comp specialist Service Insurance Companies
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon appoints Reville as MD, Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader for Risk Capital
item.description: Global broker Aon has announced that it has appointed Bob Reville as Senior Managing Director and Casualty Catastrophe Market Leader within its Risk Capital division, effective July 15th. This is an exciting step forward in strengthening how we bring together casualty expertise, analytics, and market innovation to solve increasingly complex client challenges.” Addressing his new role, Reville said: “We are building a casualty catastrophe market for latent risk that can match the property catastrophe market, expanding coverage and opening new opportunities across the insurance and capital markets. Aon’s ability to connect capital to risk supports this ambition, and I’m excited to work alongside such entrepreneurial, client-focused colleagues.” In the past, Reville has noted that the capital markets has historically avoided the casualty space due to the extremely long-tail nature of the risks, which can tie up invested capital for decades.

item.title: Long-term El Niño (ENSO) correlation holds little predictive power for annual property losses: KCC
item.description: According to a recent paper by catastrophe risk modeler Karen Clark & Company (KCC), while a long-term correlation exists between El Niño/Southern Oscillation (ENSO) phases and insured property losses, the climate pattern holds relatively little predictive power for any single year. The El Niño/Southern Oscillation (ENSO) is a climate pattern that involves sea-surface temperature changes in the eastern tropical Pacific Ocean. In its paper, KCC observes that sea-surface temperatures in the tropical Eastern Pacific have become anomalously warm in recent months, which heavily indicates the El Niño phase of ENSO.

item.title: Correa promoted to President & CEO of Munich Re Canada (Life)
item.description: Global reinsurer Munich Re has announced the appointment of Michael Correa as President and CEO of its Canada (Life) business, effective September 1, 2026. His appointment to lead our Life & Health business in Europe and Latin America speaks to the strength of our global leadership team.” Correa, who currently serves as Senior Vice President, Reinsurance Solutions for Munich Re, Canada (Life), added: “I am honoured to take on this role and to lead the Canada (Life) business at such an important time. In his new position, based in Toronto, Correa will be responsible for the Life business in Canada and the Caribbean, and will be reporting to Mari-Lizette Malherbe, Member of the Board of Management.

item.title: More favourable reinsurance conditions strengthen insurers ahead of 2026 hurricane season: KBRA
item.description: KBRA, the US-based global credit ratings agency, says that more favourable reinsurance conditions are providing insurers with stronger protection ahead of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, despite warning that a quieter seasonal outlook does not remove the threat of significant catastrophe losses. January, April, and mid-year 2026 renewal reports from brokers have highlighted that abundant capacity from both traditional reinsurers and insurance-linked securities (ILS) investors has driven more favourable renewal outcomes for cedents, with KBRA-rated insurers reporting property catastrophe reinsurance price reductions of between 10% and 25% at the June 2026 renewals. While forecasts indicate below-average Atlantic hurricane activity in 2026, KBRA stresses that seasonal projections cannot predict landfall, meaning a single hurricane affecting a heavily insured coastal region could still result in substantial insured losses.

item.title: MS Re hires Lisa Butera as CUO for NA and expands Lucas Castagnino’s leadership role
item.description: Switzerland-based reinsurer MS Reinsurance has hired Lisa Butera from Swiss Re to serve as Chief Underwriting Officer (CUO) for North America (NA), and has also expanded Lucas Castagnino’s role to CUO for Latin America and Caribbean. Lisa’s and Lucas’s experience, leadership skills and perspectives will help us build on the momentum we have already created.” “These appointments follow a comprehensive search process, which included both internal and external candidates, highlighting the strength and depth of talent within MS Re’s underwriting organization. Butera brings more than three decades of leadership experience across insurance and reinsurance markets to MS Re.
